examples.title: Basic CREATE TABLE
examples.description: Creating a simple Student table with common constraints
examples.code:
CREATE TABLE STUDENT (
    StudentID INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    Name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    Email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
    Age INT CHECK (Age >= 18 AND Age <= 25),
    Course VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Computer Science',
    CreatedAt TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
examples.explanation: This creates a STUDENT table with StudentID as auto-incrementing primary key, Name cannot be NULL, Email must be unique, Age is validated between 18-25, Course defaults to 'Computer Science', and CreatedAt automatically timestamps.

examples.title: CREATE TABLE with Foreign Key
examples.description: Creating related tables for order management system
examples.code:
CREATE TABLE CUSTOMER (
    CustomerID INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    Name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    Email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    Phone VARCHAR(20)
);

CREATE TABLE ORDERS (
    OrderID INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    CustomerID INT NOT NULL,
    OrderDate DATE NOT NULL,
    TotalAmount DECIMAL(10,2) CHECK (TotalAmount > 0),
    Status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'Pending',
    FOREIGN KEY (CustomerID) 
        REFERENCES CUSTOMER(CustomerID)
        ON DELETE CASCADE
);
examples.explanation: CUSTOMER table stores customer data with unique email. ORDERS table has foreign key referencing CUSTOMER. ON DELETE CASCADE ensures when a customer is deleted, all their orders are automatically deleted.

examples.title: Composite Primary Key
examples.description: Creating enrollment table with composite key
examples.code:
CREATE TABLE ENROLLMENT (
    StudentID INT NOT NULL,
    CourseID INT NOT NULL,
    EnrollmentDate DATE NOT NULL,
    Grade VARCHAR(2),
    PRIMARY KEY (StudentID, CourseID),
    FOREIGN KEY (StudentID) REFERENCES STUDENTS(StudentID),
    FOREIGN KEY (CourseID) REFERENCES COURSES(CourseID)
);
examples.explanation: ENROLLMENT table uses a composite primary key (StudentID + CourseID) because a student can enroll in multiple courses and a course can have multiple students. This ensures each student-course combination is unique.

examples.title: ALTER TABLE Operations
examples.explanation: ALTER TABLE modifies existing table structure. Can add/drop columns, modify data types, add/remove constraints. These operations change the table schema without losing existing data.
